title: Fix failing unit tests
body: These changes fix the failing tests introduced by https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/pull/18450

step_plan.summary: Align ResultStore key handling by introducing and using a single canonical key for metadata reads/writes, cache entries, and lock checks across create_result_record, _persist_result_record, _exists, and _read, and simplify _resolved_key_path to be deterministic. These changes restore consistent behavior so unit tests around ResultRecord/result persistence pass.

steps.reason: Provide a single deterministic canonicalization for storage/cache/lock keys. Avoid double-calling _resolve_path and ensure the method returns the exact canonical path string used by other methods for cache keys and metadata key derivation.
steps.side_effects: Alters the canonical form used across ResultStore; tests that depend on previously inconsistent behavior will be fixed. Storage backends that relied on the old multi-call semantics must still be compatible. This is a low-level change that should not change public signatures but may affect stored metadata paths; run result-related tests and any custom StorageBlock integrations.

steps.operation: Ensure created ResultRecord uses the canonical storage key
steps.reason: Assign the ResultRecord.storage_key (or relevant storage-key field) using the canonical path returned by _resolved_key_path (or the new canonicalization helper) so that persisted metadata and in-memory records use the same key format.
steps.side_effects: ResultRecord instances will carry the canonical storage key which aligns creation and persist/read flows. Tests that assert exact storage_key strings may be impacted. Keeps public API unchanged but may change persisted metadata key naming for new writes.

steps.operation: Write metadata, cache, and lock logic using canonical key
steps.reason: Replace uses of the raw input key and self.cache[key] with the single canonical key (from _resolved_key_path) for: metadata writes to metadata_storage, cache insertions, and lock acquisition/validation. This aligns persist behavior with read/existence checks.
steps.side_effects: Persists metadata under the canonical key consistent with reads. Existing metadata written previously under different keys may not be found until read-side compatibility is addressed (but read side will be changed too). Cache semantics change to use canonical keys — in-memory cache consumers must also look up by canonical keys. Verify tests for concurrent writes/locks and cache invalidation.

steps.operation: Make existence checks use canonical key and check metadata expiration consistently
steps.reason: Compute and use the same canonical key that persist uses when reading metadata_storage and when checking lock/cache state. Also ensure expiration checks use the metadata read from the canonical location so _exists returns correct results.
steps.side_effects: Fixes false negatives in existence checks caused by mismatched metadata keys. May change behavior where previously a key appeared to not exist. Downstream code that relies on the old mismatch behavior could observe different existence responses; run unit tests for result existence and expiration logic.

steps.operation: Read metadata, locks, and cache using canonical key and preserve backup_serializer logic
steps.reason: Use the canonical key for metadata reads, lock checks/waiting, and cache lookups so reads find metadata and cached ResultRecords that persist wrote. Preserve the backup_serializer fallback when reading combined payloads so deserialization behavior remains compatible with tests.
steps.side_effects: Ensures reads hit the same cache entries and metadata storage paths that persist uses, fixing deserialization/persistence tests. Any code expecting the old raw-key behavior will be affected. Must run result_record and result_store test suites; verify behavior across storage backends and concurrency/lock tests.
